disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe, constant pain fixed to the left lower abdomen for about 24 hours, progressively worsening
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the pain and when did it start?
Is the pain constant or does it come and go?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's low down on the left side of my belly, in one spot, and it's been getting worse for about a day. It's constant — a hot twisting pain that never eases off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High fever with chills, shivering and rigors accompanying the abdominal pain
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever or chills?
Have you been able to take your temperature?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I've got a high fever — the home thermometer said 38.6 — and I can't stop shivering and shaking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Change in bowel habit — no bowel motion for about four days
trigger_question_examples: When did you last open your bowels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I haven't been able to go to the toilet for about four days now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked nausea, complete loss of appetite, and inability to tolerate oral intake
trigger_question_examples: Are you feeling sick or vomiting?
Can you keep food and fluids down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel really sick and I've got no appetite at all — even the thought of food is awful, and I can't face eating or drinking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known diverticulosis found incidentally on a screening colonoscopy about three years ago, never previously symptomatic
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any known bowel conditions?
Have you had a colonoscopy before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They found little pouches in my lower bowel by chance on a screening colonoscopy about three years ago — diverticulosis — but they've never given me any trouble until now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No visible blood passed from the back passage
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any blood when you go to the toilet?
Any black or tarry stools, or blood in the bowl?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't seen any blood.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain so severe the patient is bedbound, guarding the left side and unable to stand upright, needing help to mobilise
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting what you can do?
Can you move around or stand up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't straighten up or get out of bed on my own — I'm curled up guarding my left side and I need help just to get to the bathroom.
